summary: Case shows burnout with inability to disconnect from work and rest that feels unrewarding, anchored by six changeable factors (C01–C06). I propose six modifiable predictors across BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains and their interactions: enforce off-hours boundaries (reducing work during rest), implement recovery-oriented micro-breaks and sleep optimization, cultivate self-compassion to reduce guilt, establish a stop-work ritual with timeboxing, build concrete boundary skills with scripts, and optimize social support to counter perceived minimization. Each lever targets specific criteria, has simple metrics, and is designed for a 4–8 week rollout with clear feasibility and impact estimates. The plan emphasizes actionable behavior change, cognitive reframing where needed, and social-system supports to maximize transfer and safety.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured off-work boundary protocol including scheduled auto-replies, Do Not Disturb settings, calendar blocks for rest, and weekly accountability check-ins with a peer.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly reduces work activity during designated rest periods (C01) and increases perceived control over work-life boundaries (C04); regular accountability reinforces sustainable boundary use.
solutions.measurement: % of designated rest periods with zero work activity; average rest duration; self-reported restfulness (0–10); frequency of boundary-tool usage (DND, auto-reply).
solutions.mapping_query: How does enforcing off-work boundaries via digital controls affect intrusion of work during rest (C01) and perceived control (C04)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured recovery micro-breaks and sleep hygiene optimization
solutions.solution_candidate: Guided 5–7 minute restorative breaks (breathing, light movement) plus a daily 15–20 minute rest window; sleep schedule regularization, improved light exposure in the morning, reduced blue light at night, and caffeine timing adjustments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Provides actual rest and supports circadian alignment, thereby improving perceived restoration (C02) and reducing subjective fatigue.
solutions.measurement: Restfulness rating after breaks; daily fatigue score (0–10); subjective sleep quality diary (0–10); adherence to break times.
solutions.mapping_query: Will structured micro-rests and sleep hygiene improvements increase restoration and reduce fatigue (C02)?

solutions.solution_candidate: Guided self-compassion exercises, journaling, and cognitive reframing to normalize rest and reduce guilt about taking time off.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces self-criticism and guilt during rest, increasing willingness to disengage and recover.
solutions.measurement: Guilt/self-criticism scale; self-compassion scale; rest duration intentions vs. actual rest.
solutions.mapping_query: Does increasing self-compassion and guilt-reappraisal reduce C03 (guilt during rest)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Stop-work ritual and timeboxing with accountability
solutions.solution_candidate: End-of-day stop-work ritual (plan next day, close tasks, disable after-hours email), timeboxing of remaining tasks, and accountability partner to reinforce boundaries.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases perceived control over workload (C04) and clarifies boundaries, reducing difficulty in boundary implementation (C05) via structured practice and social reinforcement.
solutions.measurement: End-of-day boundary adherence percentage; time spent on off-hours work; frequency/duration of notifications; self-rated boundary satisfaction.
solutions.mapping_query: Can stop-work rituals and accountability improve boundary adherence (C05) and perceived control (C04)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Boundary skills training and practical boundary scripts
solutions.solution_candidate: Training in boundary setting with concrete communication scripts; role-plays; adjusting notification settings and visibility to others.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances ability to set and communicate boundaries, which reduces social minimization (C06) and improves boundary feasibility (C05) through practiced, repeatable scripts.
solutions.measurement: Boundaries practiced per week; frequency of boundary-related communications; perceived quality of social response; social support questionnaire.
solutions.mapping_query: Do boundary communication skills and scripts reduce boundary difficulty (C05) and perceived social minimization (C06)?

solutions.solution_candidate: Mapping of supportive contacts; regular check-ins with a chosen supportive person; social-prescribing style linkage to community supports; psychoeducation for support network.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances perceived social support and reduces minimization by social network; strengthens belonging and accountability.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale (e.g., MSPSS); frequency of supportive interactions; mood and stress indicators; satisfaction with social network.
solutions.mapping_query: Will structured social support and education of the social network increase perceived support (C06) and reduce minimization?
